notes: Public-source v0.1 database for industrial software, OT, automation, simulation and industrial data.
This is the cross-cutting layer that connects AI, defence, aerospace and manufacturing opportunity zones.
This is a strategic dependency graph, not a supplier-contract database.
Edges marked fact_status=inferred are system-level dependency hypotheses.
Designed for Codex ingestion and website graph generation.

siemens_xcelerator.title: Siemens — Xcelerator / industrial AI / digital twin / software-defined automation
siemens_xcelerator.notes: Siemens describes AI, digital twin, software-defined automation and Xcelerator marketplace/products including NX, Teamcenter, Femap, Building X, Electrification X.

dassault_3dexperience.title: Dassault Systèmes — 3DEXPERIENCE platform
dassault_3dexperience.notes: 3DEXPERIENCE platform connects people, ideas, data and solutions; single version of truth; virtual twin; cloud; marketplace.

ptc_products.title: PTC Products
ptc_products.notes: PTC product portfolio includes Codebeamer, Modeler, RV&S, Windchill, ThingWorx, Kepware, Vuforia and Onshape-related product lines.

rockwell_factorytalk.title: Rockwell Automation — FactoryTalk
rockwell_factorytalk.notes: FactoryTalk software covers design, operations, maintenance, analytics, IIoT, AR and machine learning for industrial environments.

opcfoundation_opcua.title: OPC Foundation — OPC UA
opcfoundation_opcua.notes: OPC UA is a platform-independent architecture for industrial interoperability; supports security and machine-to-enterprise connectivity.

isa_iec_62443.title: ISA — ISA/IEC 62443 Series of Standards
isa_iec_62443.notes: ISA/IEC 62443 defines requirements/processes for secure industrial automation and control systems.

isa95.title: ISA — ISA-95 Enterprise-Control System Integration
isa95.notes: ISA-95 is the enterprise-control system integration standard used to structure ERP/MES/OT boundaries.

unified_namespace.title: HiveMQ — Unified Namespace
unified_namespace.notes: Unified Namespace reference for MQTT-based industrial data architecture.

sparkplug.title: Eclipse Sparkplug
sparkplug.notes: Sparkplug provides MQTT topic namespace and payload standards for industrial devices and applications.

autodesk_fusion.title: Autodesk Fusion
autodesk_fusion.notes: Cloud-based CAD/CAM/CAE and product design/manufacturing platform.

hexagon_msc.title: Hexagon Manufacturing Intelligence
hexagon_msc.notes: Manufacturing intelligence, metrology, design/engineering and manufacturing software.

synopsys_ansys.title: Synopsys — Ansys acquisition
synopsys_ansys.notes: Synopsys/Ansys transaction context for simulation + EDA convergence.

mathworks.title: MathWorks
mathworks.notes: MATLAB and Simulink for engineering computation, model-based design and simulation.

nvidia_omniverse.title: NVIDIA Omniverse
nvidia_omniverse.notes: Platform for 3D workflows, digital twins and industrial simulation.

schneider_ecostruxure.title: Schneider Electric EcoStruxure
schneider_ecostruxure.notes: IoT-enabled architecture and platform for energy management and automation.

aveva.title: AVEVA
aveva.notes: Industrial software for engineering, operations, data, visualization and asset performance.

abb_ability.title: ABB Ability
abb_ability.notes: ABB digital platform and industrial software/services.

honeywell_forge.title: Honeywell Forge
honeywell_forge.notes: Industrial software and operational intelligence platform.

emerson_software.title: Emerson Software
emerson_software.notes: Automation software, control, asset management and operations.

yokogawa.title: Yokogawa
yokogawa.notes: Industrial automation, control and information systems.

beckhoff.title: Beckhoff Automation
beckhoff.notes: PC-based automation, EtherCAT, PLC, motion, control and industrial PCs.

codesys.title: CODESYS
codesys.notes: IEC 61131-3 development system and automation software.

ignition.title: Ignition by Inductive Automation
ignition.notes: Industrial application platform with SCADA, IIoT, MES and reporting capabilities.

sap_digital_manufacturing.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing
sap_digital_manufacturing.notes: Manufacturing execution, insights and integration in SAP ecosystem.

nodes.name: Industrial Software / OT Stack
nodes.description: Cross-cutting industrial software, OT, automation, simulation and data stack extracted from AI, defence, aerospace and manufacturing realities.

nodes.name: CAD / CAM Layer
nodes.description: Mechanical design, manufacturing programming, NC/CNC, tooling, product geometry and engineering authoring.

nodes.name: CAE / Simulation Layer
nodes.description: Physics simulation, systems simulation, finite element analysis, CFD, electromagnetic simulation and virtual validation.

nodes.name: PLM / ALM / MBSE Layer
nodes.description: Requirements, product lifecycle, bill of materials, configuration, change management, ALM and model-based systems engineering.

nodes.name: MES / MOM Layer
nodes.description: Work orders, production execution, quality, traceability, genealogy, shop-floor performance and manufacturing operations management.

nodes.name: SCADA / HMI Layer
nodes.description: Supervisory control, operator interfaces, alarms, historian connections and plant applications.

nodes.name: PLC / Control Runtime Layer
nodes.description: PLCs, industrial controllers, runtime systems, motion control, safety systems and control programming.

nodes.name: Industrial Connectivity Layer
nodes.description: Protocol gateways, OPC UA, MQTT/Sparkplug, fieldbus bridges and IT/OT connectivity.

nodes.name: Industrial DataOps / Contextualization Layer
nodes.description: Time-series data, asset models, semantic context, namespace, event streams and operational data products.

nodes.name: Digital Twin / Virtual Twin Layer
nodes.description: Virtual twins of products, plants, lines, assets, buildings and operational systems.

nodes.name: Industrial AI / Agent Layer
nodes.description: Industrial copilots, optimization agents, anomaly detection, predictive maintenance, planning and engineering automation.

nodes.name: OT Cybersecurity Layer
nodes.description: Asset discovery, network monitoring, vulnerability management, segmentation, incident response and IEC 62443 evidence.

nodes.name: Industrial Cloud / Edge Layer
nodes.description: Industrial edge compute, cloud IoT, device management, edge workloads and data pipelines.

nodes.name: ERP / Supply Chain Integration Layer
nodes.description: ERP, supply chain planning, procurement, production planning and enterprise-to-shop-floor integration.

nodes.name: Maintenance / Reliability Layer
nodes.description: CMMS, EAM, predictive maintenance, asset performance, work orders and reliability analytics.

nodes.name: Industrial Standards / Governance Layer
nodes.description: ISA/IEC 62443, ISA-95, OPC UA, MQTT Sparkplug and governance patterns that make industrial data portable and secure.

nodes.name: Frontline Operations / Worker Layer
nodes.description: Work instructions, operator guidance, inspections, remote assistance, training, EHS and frontline apps.

nodes.name: Industrial Low-Code / App Layer
nodes.description: Low-code apps, dashboards, workflows, forms and data-driven plant applications.

nodes.name: Quality / Traceability Layer
nodes.description: Batch records, genealogy, nonconformance, serialization, audit trails and traceability across regulated manufacturing.

nodes.name: OPC UA
nodes.description: Platform-independent architecture for secure industrial interoperability and information modeling.

nodes.name: MQTT Sparkplug
nodes.description: Standard for MQTT topic namespace and payloads for industrial data producers and consumers.

nodes.name: ISA-95
nodes.description: Enterprise-control system integration standard structuring ERP, MES and control layers.

nodes.name: ISA/IEC 62443
nodes.description: Consensus-based standards for secure industrial automation and control systems.

nodes.name: Unified Namespace
nodes.description: Shared namespace architecture for contextual industrial data streams and OT/IT integration.

nodes.name: Digital Thread
nodes.description: Connected requirements, design, simulation, manufacturing, quality and service evidence across lifecycle.

nodes.name: Industrial Ontology / Asset Model
nodes.description: Common operational data model connecting assets, tags, events, processes, products and systems.

nodes.name: Siemens Xcelerator / Digital Industries Software
nodes.description: Siemens industrial software ecosystem across AI, digital twin, automation, Teamcenter, NX, Femap, Opcenter, TIA Portal and Xcelerator.

nodes.strategic_role: French virtual twin and PLM platform
nodes.description: 3DEXPERIENCE platform with CATIA, ENOVIA, DELMIA, SIMULIA, SOLIDWORKS and virtual twin ecosystem.

nodes.description: FactoryTalk covers design, operations, maintenance, analytics, IIoT, AR and ML for industrial environments.

nodes.description: Schneider EcoStruxure and AVEVA industrial software for energy management, automation, operations and data.

nodes.description: Industrial application platform for SCADA, MES, IIoT, reporting and visualization.

nodes.name: Kepware
nodes.description: PTC industrial connectivity platform for connecting devices and systems.

nodes.name: Autodesk Fusion
nodes.description: Cloud-connected product design and manufacturing platform.

nodes.name: Hexagon Manufacturing Intelligence
nodes.description: Metrology, manufacturing intelligence, engineering and operations software.

nodes.name: Ansys
nodes.description: Engineering simulation software for physics, electronics, fluids, structures and systems.

nodes.name: MathWorks MATLAB/Simulink
nodes.description: MATLAB and Simulink for engineering computation, simulation and model-based design.

nodes.name: NVIDIA Omniverse
nodes.description: 3D, digital twin and industrial simulation platform.

nodes.description: Manufacturing execution and enterprise manufacturing integration in SAP ecosystem.

nodes.description: Manufacturing data cloud and analytics layer.

nodes.description: Manufacturing data and AI platform.

nodes.name: Palantir AIP / Ontology
nodes.description: AI/ontology platform for enterprise and operational decision-making.

nodes.name: C3 AI
nodes.description: Enterprise AI applications for reliability, supply chain and industrial operations.

nodes.name: Tulip Interfaces
nodes.description: Frontline operations and low-code manufacturing apps.

nodes.name: MaintainX
nodes.description: CMMS/frontline operations and maintenance software.

nodes.name: Uptake
nodes.description: Predictive maintenance and industrial intelligence.

nodes.name: Cognite Data Fusion
nodes.description: Industrial data contextualization and operations platform.

nodes.name: Litmus
nodes.description: Industrial edge data collection and analytics platform.

nodes.name: HighByte
nodes.description: Industrial data contextualization and data operations platform.

nodes.name: Claroty
nodes.description: OT asset visibility, cyber-physical systems security and exposure management.

nodes.name: Dragos
nodes.description: Industrial cybersecurity, OT threat intelligence and incident response.

nodes.name: Nozomi Networks
nodes.description: OT and IoT security monitoring.

nodes.name: Armis
nodes.strategic_role: Cyber exposure and asset intelligence vendor
nodes.description: Cyber exposure management and asset intelligence for OT/IoT/IT.

nodes.name: Tenable OT
nodes.description: OT security and exposure management.

nodes.name: Brownfield Integration Gap
nodes.description: Factories contain old machines, proprietary protocols, heterogeneous PLCs and manual workflows; integration is expensive and brittle.

nodes.name: IT / OT Semantic Gap
nodes.description: Enterprise data teams often lack meaningful asset, process, unit, product and operational context for OT data.

nodes.name: Industrial Vendor Lock-in
nodes.description: Plants often depend on vendor-specific tools, proprietary formats, licenses, protocols and integrator knowledge.

nodes.name: OT Cyber Asset Visibility Gap
nodes.description: Industrial operators often lack complete OT asset inventory, vulnerability context and secure remote access visibility.

nodes.name: Industrial Data Quality Gap
nodes.description: Noisy tags, missing metadata, bad timestamps, inconsistent units and poor lineage make industrial analytics unreliable.

nodes.name: Digital Thread Breakage
nodes.description: Requirements, CAD, simulation, manufacturing, quality and service evidence break across tools and organizations.

nodes.name: Simulation-to-Shopfloor Gap
nodes.description: Simulation and digital twin outputs are often disconnected from MES, PLC, quality and operator workflows.

nodes.name: Industrial AI Trust Gap
nodes.description: Industrial AI requires evidence, guardrails, root-cause explanations, safe deployment and operator trust.

nodes.name: Frontline Adoption Gap
nodes.description: Operator adoption fails when software ignores real shop-floor workflows, offline realities, shift patterns and maintenance habits.

nodes.name: IEC 62443 Evidence Gap
nodes.description: IEC 62443 requirements create need for continuous evidence, patch status, segmentation, policies and vendor accountability.

nodes.name: Factory Energy Visibility Gap
nodes.description: Plants need machine/line/process-level energy and emissions visibility, not only utility bills or corporate reports.

nodes.name: Industrial Change Management Gap
nodes.description: Code, recipes, BOMs, PLC programs, machine parameters and process changes often lack unified audit/control.

nodes.name: EU AI Stack Reference
nodes.description: Industrial software layer supports EU AI opportunities through data, deployment, edge, governance and AI factories.

nodes.name: Global AI Stack Reference
nodes.description: Industrial software layer depends on and feeds global AI stack through industrial data, edge, inference and model routing.

nodes.name: European Defence Stack Reference
nodes.description: Industrial software layer supports defence readiness, supplier visibility, simulation, compliance and secure integration.

nodes.name: Global Aerospace Stack Reference
nodes.description: Industrial software layer supports aerospace certification, digital thread, MRO, quality and supplier visibility.

CAD/CAM artifacts need lifecycle, configuration, requirements and BOM context.
Simulation results require requirements, product structure and configuration traceability.
PLM/ALM/MBSE is the backbone of product-to-production traceability.
Manufacturing execution depends on ERP, work orders, materials and production planning.
MES depends on machine/control-level status and execution data.
SCADA/HMI depends on PLC/control runtime data and command interfaces.
Industrial DataOps depends on reliable OT connectivity and protocol translation.
Industrial AI depends on clean, contextualized, time-aligned operational data.
Digital twins need operational data, asset models and contextualization to stay connected to reality.
Digital twin use cases often depend on simulation models and engineering constraints.
Reliability software depends on asset hierarchy, telemetry, events and maintenance context.
Quality and traceability depend on production execution, genealogy and process context.
OT security depends on asset discovery, protocol visibility and network context.
Industrial cloud/edge depends on secure device and plant connectivity.
Industrial apps need contextualized data and event streams.
Frontline apps need work orders, procedures, quality and production context.

Aerospace quality and certification depend on traceability and lifecycle evidence.

opportunities.title: Industrial dependency graph OS for factories and critical suppliers
opportunities.reason: Every database so far points back to supplier visibility, digital thread, OT assets and bottlenecks. A graph OS that maps machines, suppliers, software, OT assets, data flows, compliance and production dependencies can serve aerospace, defence, energy and manufacturing.

opportunities.title: IEC 62443 continuous evidence and patch-status platform
opportunities.reason: ISA/IEC 62443 creates continuous evidence needs around IACS security. A lightweight platform can track assets, zones/conduits, patch state, vendor SBOMs, controls and audit evidence across OT environments.

opportunities.title: Unified Namespace builder and data-quality monitor
opportunities.reason: Factories need contextual OT data before AI or analytics can work. A UNS builder that validates MQTT/Sparkplug/OPC UA streams, naming, units, lineage and missing metadata attacks a real bottleneck.

opportunities.title: Brownfield machine connector marketplace
opportunities.reason: Brownfield factories are full of legacy PLCs, machines and proprietary protocols. A verified connector marketplace with templates, drivers, test harnesses and integrator reviews can reduce IT/OT integration cost.

opportunities.title: AI-ready factory score and remediation engine
opportunities.reason: Industrial AI fails without data quality, asset models, connectivity, security and operator trust. A score/remediation engine can tell plants exactly what blocks AI deployment and what to fix first.

opportunities.title: Simulation-to-MES bridge for validated production change
opportunities.reason: Simulation is powerful but often disconnected from the shop floor. A bridge from simulation/digital-twin outputs into MES, recipes, work instructions and change-control can create high-value industrial workflow software.

opportunities.title: PLC and machine-parameter change-control vault
opportunities.reason: PLC code, recipes and machine parameters often change without strong unified audit, rollback and impact analysis. A vendor-neutral change-control vault is accessible and painful.

opportunities.title: Frontline-to-digital-thread work instruction platform
opportunities.reason: Operators and technicians generate real evidence, deviations, fixes and tribal knowledge. Connecting frontline work instructions to PLM/MES/quality records can close digital-thread gaps without replacing big systems.

opportunities.title: OT asset intelligence for procurement and cyber insurance
opportunities.reason: OT asset data is valuable beyond security: procurement, lifecycle planning, insurance, maintenance and modernization. A normalized asset intelligence layer can sit on top of security discovery tools.

opportunities.title: Machine-level energy and emissions digital twin
opportunities.reason: Factories need energy visibility at asset/line/product level. A machine-level energy/emissions twin can connect meters, machines, work orders and product carbon reporting.

opportunities.title: Industrial vendor-lock-in translator and migration assistant
opportunities.reason: Factories struggle to migrate between vendor stacks because tags, recipes, screens, PLC code, reports and histories are trapped. A translator/migration assistant can reduce switching friction.

opportunities.title: Industrial software opportunity engine for founders and investors
opportunities.reason: This cross-cutting layer exposes where AI, defence, aerospace and manufacturing all hit the same bottlenecks: data context, traceability, compliance, OT security, simulation-to-shopfloor and supplier visibility.
